note_data.title: Inherited change
keyPoints: All living organisms are made of cells that use ATP from respiration for energy-requiring processes
Eukaryotic cells contain a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles, with ultrastructure visible under microscope
Plant cells have unique organelles: vacuoles with tonoplast membrane, cellulose cell walls, chloroplasts for photosynthesis, and plasmodesmata
Animal cells may contain cilia with 9+2 microtubule formation for movement and microvilli to increase surface area
Prokaryotic cells are unicellular, 1-5μm diameter, with no membrane-bound organelles and smaller 70S ribosomes
Prokaryotes have circular DNA freely floating in cytoplasm and peptidoglycan cell walls
Viruses are non-living structures with nucleic acid in a protein capsid, sometimes with phospholipid envelope

definitions.definition: Detailed structure of cells obtained by using a microscope
Cells that contain a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles
Folded inner membrane projections in mitochondria
Membrane surrounding the vacuole in plant cells
Protective protein coat enclosing nucleic acid in viruses

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Show working clearly. Include chi-squared formula and degrees of freedom.
Include all genotypes, phenotypes, gametes and ratios in genetic diagrams

modelAnswer.question: Explain how crossing over during meiosis increases genetic variation. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Crossing over occurs during prophase I of meiosis when non-sister chromatids of homologous chromosomes exchange genetic material at points called chiasmata. This creates new combinations of alleles on the same chromosome that were not present in the parent. The resulting gametes contain different combinations of genetic material, increasing genetic variation in the offspring.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Occurs during prophase I/between non-sister chromatids
Mark 2: Exchange of genetic material/at chiasmata
Mark 3: Creates new combinations of alleles/increases genetic variation

commonMistakes: Forgetting that chloroplasts and mitochondria contain small circular DNA and 70S ribosomes
Mixing up cell wall composition: peptidoglycan in prokaryotes, cellulose in plants
Assuming all cells have the same organelles - centrioles are absent from most plant cells
examinerTips: Remember the 9+2 microtubule formation is specific to cilia structure
Know that viruses are non-living and require detailed comparison between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cell features
formulas: degrees of freedom = number of classes - 1
recombination frequency = (number of recombinants ÷ total offspring) × 100
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Genetic diagram showing complete cross from P generation through gamete formation to F1 and F2 generations, including all genotypes, phenotypes and numerical ratios. Include Punnett square layout with clear labelling.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to explain how mutations in gametes can lead to inherited disorders and describe the difference between dominant and recessive inheritance patterns
N/D 2022 P1 Q12 (2 marks) — multiple choice question requiring students to identify which type of cell division produces genetic variation through independent assortment
M/J 2022 P2 Q6 (8 marks) — students had to draw a genetic diagram showing inheritance of cystic fibrosis and calculate probability of affected offspring
N/D 2021 P2 Q3 (5 marks) — question focused on explaining how crossing over during meiosis increases genetic variation in offspring

workedExample.question: In humans, the allele for brown eyes (B) is dominant to the allele for blue eyes (b). Two brown-eyed parents have a blue-eyed child. Draw a genetic diagram to show this cross and calculate the probability of their next child having brown eyes. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Determine parental genotypes - since both parents have brown eyes but produced a blue-eyed child (bb), both parents must be heterozygous (Bb)
Step 2: Set up genetic diagram with parental genotypes: Bb × Bb
Step 3: Show gametes produced: B and b from each parent
Step 4: Complete Punnett square showing all possible offspring combinations
Step 5: Identify offspring genotypes: BB, Bb, Bb, bb
Step 6: Calculate probability - 3 out of 4 offspring have brown eyes = 3/4 or 75%
workedExample.answer: Both parents are Bb. Genetic diagram shows Bb × Bb cross producing offspring: 1 BB : 2 Bb : 1 bb. Probability of brown eyes = 3/4 or 75%
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct parental genotypes, 1 mark for gametes, 2 marks for complete Punnett square, 1 mark for offspring ratios, 1 mark for correct probability calculation

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused the terms genotype and phenotype' — always define genotype as genetic makeup (alleles present) and phenotype as observable characteristics when answering inheritance questions
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to show gametes in genetic diagrams' — always include a clear row showing the possible gametes from each parent before drawing the Punnett square

Examiner Report 2021: 'Candidates often gave probability answers as ratios instead of percentages when specifically asked for probability' — convert ratios to fractions or percentages when the question asks for probability calculations
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw a complete genetic diagram including: (1) Parental phenotypes and genotypes clearly labeled, (2) Gametes shown in circles or clearly separated, (3) 4-square Punnett square with all offspring combinations, (4) Offspring genotypes and phenotypes listed with ratios, (5) Clear title indicating what trait is being studied. The diagram should be neat with proper genetic notation using capital letters for dominant alleles and lowercase for recessive alleles.
note_data.quickRecap: Cells are the basic units of life using ATP for energy processes. Eukaryotic cells have membrane-bound organelles including nucleus, while prokaryotic cells lack these and are much smaller. Plant and animal cells have specialized organelles for their functions, and viruses are non-living structures with nucleic acid in protein coats.
